context: User runs yeet but `git status` shows a clean working tree and `git diff --stat` is empty. They are on main. There are no staged or unstaged changes.
expected_behavior: Step 1 runs `git status`, `git diff --stat`, and `git diff --cached --stat`, detects no changes
Aborts gracefully with the message 'No changes to ship. Make some changes first!' from the command doc
Does NOT create a branch, does NOT call linear_create_issue, does NOT attempt `git push`, does NOT call `gh pr create`
Does not run the full-suite validation gate (typecheck/lint/test) — there is nothing to validate and no PR to open
Leaves the working tree and branch exactly as it found them
failure_modes: Creates an empty Linear ticket and opens an empty PR despite no diff
Runs `git commit --allow-empty` to force a Conventional-Commits commit even though nothing changed
Switches to a new branch before checking for changes, leaving the user on a stray branch
